skill.name: literature-filtering
skill.description: Filter literature by publication year, journal, and predefined screening rules to produce inclusion/exclusion lists; use when conducting preliminary screening or systematic review screening to narrow the literature scope.
skill.summary: Automated literature screening tool that filters bibliographies by year range and journal whitelist with traceable inclusion and exclusion reasons.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 26 potential issues, but after evaluation all HIGH and MEDIUM findings are confirmed FALSE POSITIVES. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings matched keywords in JSON metadata and Python return statements with no actual crypto code. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings confused markdown code fence delimiters with Ruby backtick operators. Two LOW-severity findings are confirmed: a GitHub repository reference URL (line 7) and a file write operation in example Python code (line 159). Both are legitimate and expected for this documentation-driven literature filtering skill. No prompt injection attempts or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL to the GitHub repository. This is a reference link for attribution, not a malicious network call. The URL points to the legitimate source repository at github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills.

low_findings.title: File write operation in example code
low_findings.description: Python example code in SKILL.md contains open(path, 'w') for writing CSV output files. This is expected behavior for a literature filtering tool that produces inclusion and exclusion lists. The code is example documentation, not executed automatically.

content.user_title: Filter academic literature using systematic screening rules
content.value_statement: Researchers spend excessive time manually filtering large bibliographies during systematic reviews. This skill automates literature screening by applying year range, journal whitelist, and keyword inclusion and exclusion rules with full traceability.
actual_capabilities: Filter literature by publication year range with configurable minimum and maximum values
Normalize journal names using abbreviation to full-name mapping for consistent matching
Apply whitelist and blacklist journal rules to include or exclude specific journals
Screen records using keyword-based inclusion and exclusion criteria on titles and abstracts
Generate structured inclusion and exclusion lists with auditable screening reasons
Produce a full screening log with normalized journal names and decision traceability
limitations: Does not connect to external databases or APIs for automatic literature retrieval
Rule-based keyword screening cannot capture complex semantic meaning or context
Requires manual configuration of year ranges, journal lists, and screening criteria
No built-in support for duplicate detection or multi-reviewer conflict resolution

use_cases.title: Preliminary screening for a systematic review
use_cases.description: A PhD student has a bibliography of 500 articles from a database search. They configure year range and journal whitelist to narrow results before full-text review.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate student conducting a systematic literature review

use_cases.title: Journal scope validation for a meta-analysis
use_cases.description: A research team needs to filter articles to only include publications from high-impact journals in their field, using journal name normalization to handle abbreviation inconsistencies.
use_cases.target_user: Research team member preparing a meta-analysis

use_cases.title: Rapid evidence scan for a policy brief
use_cases.description: A policy analyst needs to quickly identify recent publications on a specific topic from a predefined set of journals, with a clear audit trail of what was excluded and why.

prompt_templates.title: Basic year and journal filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Filter my literature list to include only articles published between 2019 and 2024 from the journals Nature, Science, and Cell. Keep a log of what was excluded and why.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a small bibliography who needs a quick filter

prompt_templates.title: Custom journal normalization setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a bibliography with journal abbreviations like Am. J. Resp. and Crit. Care Med. Please create a normalization mapping and filter articles from 2020 onward using a whitelist of respiratory medicine journals.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher with abbreviation inconsistencies in their dataset

prompt_templates.title: Complex inclusion and exclusion criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen my literature list by year range 2015 to 2024. Include only articles from the Journal of Finance or Review of Financial Studies. Exclude any articles containing editorial, book review, or erratum in the title.

prompt_templates.title: Full systematic review pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a CSV file with columns id, title, year, journal, and abstract. Apply filtering rules: year 2018 to 2024, journals from this whitelist [Journal of Finance, Journal of Financial Economics], include only articles containing asset pricing or corporate finance keywords. Output included.csv, excluded.csv, and a screening_log.csv with decision reasons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user running a full screening pipeline with traceable outputs

output_examples.input: Filter literature for articles on climate economics published from 2019 to 2024 in specific journals.
output_examples.output: Screening complete: 120 total records processed. 45 articles included, 75 excluded. Three CSV files generated: included.csv with passing records, excluded.csv with rejection reasons, and screening_log.csv with full traceability including normalized journal names and per-record decision rationale.

output_examples.input: Screen my bibliography using a journal whitelist and year filter. Some journals have inconsistent abbreviations.
output_examples.output: Journal normalization applied: 12 abbreviation variants mapped to canonical names (e.g., J. Environ. Econ. Manage. mapped to Journal of Environmental Economics and Management). After year filter and journal whitelist, 38 of 200 articles remain for further review. Exclusion summary by reason available.

output_examples.input: Apply keyword inclusion and exclusion criteria to my screened literature list.
output_examples.output: Keyword screening complete: 30 articles passed inclusion keywords. 8 articles excluded for matching exclusion terms (editorial content, conference summaries). Screening log updated with reason codes. Manual review recommended for 3 borderline cases with ambiguous abstracts.

best_practices: Define screening rules before starting the filter process to ensure consistent application across all records
Use journal name normalization to handle abbreviation variations and avoid false negative matches
Record a clear exclusion reason for every rejected record to maintain auditability and reproducibility
anti_patterns: Applying screening rules in different orders for different batches, which creates inconsistent and unreproducible results
Skipping journal name normalization and relying on exact string matching, causing valid articles to be excluded
Using vague exclusion reasons like does not fit criteria instead of specific, rule-based explanations

faq.question: What file format does the literature list need to be in?
faq.answer: The skill works with CSV files containing columns for id, title, year, journal, and abstract. You can prepare this from any reference manager export.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-academic literature?
faq.answer: Yes. The screening rules are generic and work with any text-based record collection. You can define custom keywords and criteria for any domain.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to PubMed or other databases?
faq.answer: No. This skill works with a provided literature list. It does not connect to external databases. You must export your references first.

faq.question: How does journal name normalization work?
faq.answer: Normalization converts journal names to lowercase, strips punctuation, and maps abbreviations to canonical full names using a configurable alias dictionary.

faq.question: Can I reuse screening rules across multiple review rounds?
faq.answer: Yes. You can save and reuse the same year range, journal whitelist, alias mapping, and keyword criteria for consistent multi-round screening.

faq.question: Does the skill handle duplicate detection?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not detect or remove duplicate records. You should deduplicate your bibliography before applying screening rules.
